MOTION / Notice Of Motion To Seal Records Lodged In Support Of Opposition To Defendants' Motion To Stay Or Dismiss Verified Complaint
Off Calendar Nov-17-2021 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances via Zoom Conference
Elizabeth Sandza, Esq, 415-273-9595, elizabeth@sandzalaw.com, for Plaintiff W. Scott Harkonen, M.D.
Elizabeth K. McCloskey, Esq., Keker, Van Nest & Peters LLP, 415-3915400, emccloskey@keker.com, for Defendants Intermune Inc et al.
Not reported.

No argument is held. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PLAINTIFF W. SCOTT HARKONEN, M.D.'S Motion To Seal Records Lodged In Support Of Opposition To Defendants' Motion To Stay Or Dismiss Verified Complaint.

Plaintiff's unopposed motion to seal records lodged in support of Plaintiff's opposition to defendants' motion to stay or dismiss verified complaint is granted. No opposition filed and the motion complies with California Rules of Court, Rules 2.550 and 2.551. The Court finds that there exists an overriding interest that overcomes the right of public access to the portions of the records to be sealed; the overriding interest supports sealing those records; a substantial probability exists that the overriding interest will be prejudiced if the record is not sealed; the proposed sealing is narrowly tailored; and no less restrictive means exist to achieve the overriding interest.

Order is signed.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman; Clerk: W. Trupek; Not Reported (302/EPS)
